text:
There was an old catfish who fancied himself quite a dancer.

Every evening, he pranced around his quiet home on the riverbank. Fishermen chuckled as they watched him shake his fiskey tail. They’d toss him breadcrumbs, hoping he might swallow some.

But he didn't. He would twitch his whiskers thoughtfully and swim back.

One fisherman decided to teach him. "You go dance some for me!" he called.

So off flew specks of fishmeal.

A fortnight passed.

One evening, something fluttered past.

That was the old catfish. With a hop?

With a shimmy? That didn't seem likely.

Perhaps just a dream?

It fluttered toward him.

It fluttered right into his fins.

A big laugh escaped the fisherman.

The old catfish lifted his head.

They stared each other down.

That's nothing!

With a graceful leap...

It flew off into the sunset.

They saw it. Did he leap?

Or
